ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT code 226934003 is a closest_parent match for beef sirloin steak; the lean-only specification is not separately coded within SNOMED CT at this level of granularity.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of raw lean beef sirloin steak is approximately 150 g, equivalent to one medium steak portion before cooking.

nutrient_source.title: Beef, loin, top sirloin steak, separable lean only, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for beef top sirloin, separable lean only, raw is an exact match for this record. HPB Singapore values (energy 115 kcal, protein 24.1 g, fat 1.9 g, carbohydrate 0.0 g, sodium 57 mg, potassium 380 mg, calcium 6 mg, iron 2.2 mg, cholesterol 58 mg) are consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for lean raw beef sirloin and are retained as the primary nutrient basis. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and additional micronutrients are supplemented from USDA SR Legacy (USDA FDC ID 174032).

gi_source.title: N/A — Glycemic index not applicable for negligible-carbohydrate foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: Beef sirloin steak (raw, lean) contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. The numeric value 0 is used as a sentinel to satisfy the requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric; gi_category is set to not_applicable to convey that no true GI measurement exists for this food. GL per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (57 mg/100 g) and high potassium (380 mg/100 g) yield a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio that supports blood pressure regulation.
hypertension.guidance: Lean raw beef sirloin is suitable for hypertensive individuals when prepared without added salt, high-sodium marinades, or sauces; limit cooked portion to 100–120 g to maintain overall dietary sodium targets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar content means no glycaemic impact; high protein (24.1 g/100 g) supports satiety and lean muscle maintenance.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Lean beef sirloin is an appropriate protein source for people with Type 2 Diabetes; avoid high-sugar marinades or sauces (teriyaki, BBQ, hoisin) during preparation and pair with non-starchy vegetables to maintain a low overall glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat is low at approximately 0.7 g/100 g and total fat is only 1.9 g/100 g; however, a small amount of trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g, naturally occurring ruminant trans fat) is present.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lean beef sirloin can be included in a heart-healthy diet in moderate portions (100–150 g cooked); choose dry-heat cooking methods (grilling, broiling) without added saturated fats, and limit consumption to 2–3 times per week in the context of a varied diet rich in plant-based foods and oily fish.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat contains arachidonic acid (omega-6 fatty acid) that can promote pro-inflammatory cytokine production (TNF-alpha, IL-6) relevant to RA disease activity.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Limit red meat consumption to no more than 1–2 times per week for RA management; prefer oily fish or plant-based proteins as primary protein sources and pair with anti-inflammatory foods such as turmeric, ginger, and leafy greens when consuming.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beef sirloin steak (raw, lean) is uncooked red meat sourced from the rear sirloin primal of beef cattle, with all visible and separable external and internal fat removed, leaving only lean muscle tissue. The raw meat displays a deep red colour with minimal marbling due to fat trimming. Per 100 g, it provides 115 kcal, 24.1 g of high-quality complete protein containing all essential amino acids, only 1.9 g total fat (of which approximately 0.7 g is saturated fat), and 0.0 g carbohydrate, making it a very high-protein, low-fat, zero-carbohydrate food. Sodium is low at 57 mg/100 g while potassium is high at 380 mg/100 g, supporting a favourable electrolyte profile. It is a rich source of haem iron (2.2 mg/100 g), zinc (4.3 mg/100 g), selenium (27 mcg/100 g), vitamin B12 (2.4 mcg/100 g), and niacin (6.8 mg/100 g). Cholesterol is moderate at 58 mg/100 g. The glycemic index is not applicable as the food contains negligible carbohydrates. Clinically, this cut is well-suited for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes (no glycaemic impact), hypertension (low sodium, high potassium), and those seeking lean protein for weight management or muscle preservation. It is classified as a raw, minimally processed food. Halal status applies when sourced from appropriately certified suppliers. Common preparation methods include grilling, pan-searing, broiling, and stir-frying after slicing.

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol) are sourced from HPB Singapore at 0.95 confidence and are consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for beef top sirloin, separable lean only, raw (FDC ID 174032). Supplementary micronutrient values (saturated fat, B vitamins, minerals, water) are drawn directly from USDA SR Legacy, an approved primary source. The food identity is unambiguous and the source match is exact.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as beef sirloin is a pure animal muscle protein with no carbohydrate content; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as animal muscle tissue contains no dietary fibre. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae set to -1: not reported in HPB Singapore database for this entry; USDA SR Legacy reports negligible amounts for lean raw sirloin but a precise value was not available for direct mapping. Vitamin_d_mcg set to -1: not reported in HPB Singapore database; USDA SR Legacy reports trace amounts but a precise value was not confirmed for this specific lean-only cut. Vitamin_e_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB Singapore database for this entry. Trans fat of approximately 0.1 g/100 g is naturally occurring ruminant conjugated linoleic acid (CLA) and vaccenic acid, not industrially produced trans fat; this is documented in USDA SR Legacy data. Glycemic index is set to numeric 0 as a sentinel value to satisfy the requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric; gi_category is set to not_applicable to convey that no true GI measurement exists for this zero-carbohydrate food (0.0 g/100 g). Halal status is conditional on sourcing from a certified halal-slaughtered beef supplier. Ontology: FoodOn URI and FoodEx2 code are defensible matches for raw lean beef sirloin; SNOMED CT code 226934003 is a closest_parent match as lean-only specification is not separately coded in SNOMED CT.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI provided for beef sirloin steak (raw). FoodEx2 code A0CKL for beef sirloin raw is a defensible match. SNOMED CT 226934003 is a 'closest_parent' match with gap note documented. No codes are invented.

Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' is correctly used.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record passes all 6 hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1–Q8 scorecard. Primary nutrient data from HPB Singapore is consistent with USDA SR Legacy for lean raw beef sirloin and is supplemented with micronutrient data from USDA SR Legacy. Zero-carbohydrate macros are justified as naturally zero. Glycemic index is set to numeric 0 as a sentinel value with gi_category not_applicable, satisfying the numeric requirement while correctly conveying that GI is not applicable for this zero-carbohydrate food. All three mandatory health conditions are present with clinical specificity. Ontology anchors are defensible. Aliases are comprehensive and internally consistent. No mandatory non-English language requirement applies to this Western generic food.
